assertions.expectation: Explains that clusters on Regular channel will be force-upgraded to the next minor version (1.31) when 1.30 hits EoS
assertions.reason: Response clearly states that Regular channel clusters will have control plane and node pools auto-upgraded to 1.31 when 1.30 reaches EoS

assertions.expectation: Explains that clusters on Extended channel get up to 24 months of support, so 1.30 EoS is significantly later — they have more time
assertions.reason: Response explains Extended channel provides 24 months total support (14 months standard + extended period) and that minor upgrades are not automated during extended support period, giving more time

assertions.expectation: Explains that legacy 'No channel' clusters: control plane EoS minor versions are auto-upgraded to next supported minor, AND EoS node pools are force-upgraded even when 'no auto-upgrade' is configured
assertions.reason: Response specifically states that No channel clusters have control plane force-upgraded to 1.31 and node pools force-upgraded even if auto-upgrade is disabled

assertions.expectation: Mentions that a 'no upgrades' maintenance exclusion (30 days) can temporarily defer even EoS force-upgrades
assertions.reason: Response includes Option 4 showing the 'no_upgrades' exclusion command and explicitly states it can defer EoS enforcement for 30 days max

assertions.expectation: Recommends proactive upgrade planning rather than waiting for EoS enforcement
assertions.reason: Response strongly recommends Option 1 (proactive manual upgrades) and provides detailed timeline recommendations encouraging upgrades before EoS enforcement

assertions.expectation: Mentions GKE EoS notifications via Cloud Logging as a planning tool
assertions.reason: Response does not mention Cloud Logging notifications as a planning tool for EoS events

assertions.expectation: Mentions the deprecation insights dashboard for checking API compatibility before the forced upgrade
assertions.reason: Response includes in the checklist: 'Check deprecated API usage: GKE console → Insights → "Deprecations and Issues"'

assertions.expectation: Warns that 'snowflaking' (manually freezing versions) should be avoided — it creates security and reliability risks
assertions.reason: Response does not explicitly mention 'snowflaking' or warn about manually freezing versions and associated security/reliability risks
